name: Sourest Sourdough
recipeInstructions:
1. Set the sourdough starter out on a counter and bring it to room temperature. Feed as you normally do, allowing it to become foamy and active. This will take 8–12 hours.
2. Place all ingredients in the machine’s dough cycle. When the cycle is complete, remove the dough and gently squeeze out gases.
3. Cover with a damp towel and let the dough rest for 20–30 minutes. (This rest period makes the dough more pliable.)
4. Sprinkle cornmeal on a board and shape the dough into two cylindrical loaves. Place the loaves on a cornmeal‑covered baking sheet or in a special “baguette pan” purchased at a kitchen store.
5. Cover again with the damp towel and place in the refrigerator for 12–24 hours.
6. Take the dough out of the refrigerator, sprinkle lightly with water, and let it sit until fully risen (it will usually rise slightly while in the refrigerator as well).
7. Lightly spray with water and bake at 375°F for 30 minutes or until brown and crusty. For a crisper crust, spray the loaf with additional water every five minutes during baking.
8. NOTE: Each batch may vary in flavor (sourness) and texture due to temperature, humidity, and starter activity. Adjust sugar or substitute vinegar for some water to change the taste. Using flat beer instead of water can also create variations.
PLEASE NOTE: Depending on conditions, the bread will differ from batch to batch—but it is always good. The special baguette pan helps maintain shape and is well worth the cost.
